about us

a small team, a large ambition

we build tools for product teams who want to ship consistent, beautiful software — without a ten-person design-ops department.

2018

founded in brooklyn

42

team members, 18 cities

4,200

teams in production

$42M

arr, trailing 12 months

what we believe

three principles, not fifty

we keep the list short on purpose. long lists turn into wallpaper.

speed is a feature

every interaction has a target latency and we hit it or we cut it. "slow but pretty" is a failure mode.

honesty beats polish

we publish post-mortems, latency numbers, and pricing math in public. no asterisks, no wait-and-see footers.

small team, full access

every engineer takes support rotation. every customer can reach a real person. no tier-1 deflection scripts.

the team

forty-two people, eighteen cities

a sample of the faces you'll meet on support calls, in our slack community, and at meet-ups.

view the full team
our story

ten years, one mission

the milestones that shaped the product, and the lessons we keep.

2018

we start with a free figma file

mira and tariq publish the first 200-component library on a friday night. it hits 10k downloads in four days.

2019

pro launches

paid tier at $129 one-off. first 30 customers in 72 hours. the support inbox becomes a company.

2020

react components ship

a year of rewriting the library in react. 294 .tsx files, fully typed, tree-shakable.

2021

seed round (reluctantly)

$3.5m from a small group of operators. terms match founder-friendly defaults. no board seat given up.

2022

token pipeline v1

figma ↔ code two-way sync. the year our support volume halves.

2023

soc 2 type ii

compliance path unlocked. first enterprise customers sign.

2024

platform year

api, cli, webhooks, public changelog engine. untitled becomes a substrate, not a file.

2025

10k teams, profitably

broke even without taking another round. team grew to 42, retention at 92% year-over-year.

2026

v3 ships

real-time co-edit, ai token suggestions, public changelog engine — all included on every paid plan.

press & awards
the verge smashing mag fast company wired A.List
offices

three hubs, one remote-first culture

optional spaces for people who want them. nobody has to use them.

primary

brooklyn

125 atlantic ave, fifth floor. 22 desks. rooftop terrace with a questionable kettle.

secondary

lisbon

rua da boavista 104. 12 desks. the eu presence, plus unreasonable natural light.

secondary

kyoto

gion east. 6 desks. the ap overlap window plus excellent walking-meeting weather.

join us

we hire across engineering, design, and support. async-first, remote-friendly, with overlap windows per timezone.

read the handbook open roles
